name: Golden Jubilee Mine
short_description: Historic gold prospect on BLM land in the Trinity Alps region. Pan for gold and hunt quartz specimens on gentle slopes with year-round access and no permits required.
description:
Golden Jubilee Mine is a public BLM rockhounding site located in the remote Trinity County region of northern California, near the small community of Coffee. This historic gold prospecting area sits at 3,065 feet elevation on gently sloping terrain, making it accessible to rockhounds of varying skill levels. The site has been worked intermittently since the California Gold Rush era, with small-scale panning and prospect work still productive today.

The geology of this area reflects the Klamath Mountains province, where Paleozoic and Mesozoic metamorphic rocks host quartz veins carrying gold mineralization. The gentle slopes and weathered outcrops expose quartz specimens and allow streambed panning for fine gold. This location sits within a region of active geological processes where erosion continuously exposes new material.

Visitors can expect to find small flakes and dust gold in stream gravels, as well as nice quartz specimens in the surrounding hillsides. The terrain is relatively open with scattered brush, and the gentle slope makes walking and prospecting straightforward. Many collectors focus on small-scale panning in nearby drainages where heavier gold tends to accumulate.

As a BLM site, Golden Jubilee Mine offers free access under personal use guidelines. No special permits are required, though collection is strictly limited to 25 pounds per day. The remote location means you should come fully prepared with water, tools, and supplies - services are minimal in the Coffee area.

This is an excellent location for beginners interested in gold panning or anyone seeking authentic Gold Rush-era prospecting terrain without fees or restrictions.
history: This area lies within the northern California gold belt that produced significant discoveries during the 1850s-1890s Gold Rush. The quartz veins and gold mineralization here were formed during Mesozoic metamorphism and hydrothermal circulation in the Klamath Mountains, with subsequent erosion continually exposing fresh material in streambeds and hillside exposures.
tips: Focus panning efforts in drainage channels and benches where water naturally concentrates heavy materials - look for concentrations of black sand which often accompanies fine gold. Bring a 10-14 inch gold pan and classifier to process stream gravels efficiently. Quartz specimens with visible mineralization are common in exposed banks and hillside outcrops - look for specimens with iron staining or glassy clarity. Early morning and late afternoon light helps spot quartz veins in the surrounding slopes.
terrain_notes: Elevation 3,065 feet with gentle slopes and scattered brush vegetation. Open terrain with minimal tree cover allows easy foot access across hillsides. Walking distance from parking to productive areas is typically under 0.5 miles. Footing is stable on the gentle slopes with loose rock present but not treacherous. No water readily available at the site - bring your own.
best_season: May - October. Higher elevations in Trinity County can receive snow November through April, making access difficult. May through October offers reliable dry weather and passable road conditions. Summer temperatures are moderate due to elevation.
written_directions: From Coffee, take Highway 299 northeast for approximately 8 miles. Watch for signs or landmarks indicating access to Trinity Alps foothills. Turn northeast on Forest Service Road 30N50 (or local county road) and follow for approximately 2-3 miles to the Golden Jubilee prospect area. Park at any safe pullout near the mine workings. High-clearance vehicle recommended due to rougher road sections.
road_conditions: Unpaved forest service road with rough sections. High-clearance vehicle recommended. Passable in dry conditions May through October.
parking_notes: Informal pullouts near the mine site. No developed parking area. Park off-road in safe, flat locations.
collection_rules: Personal use only. Maximum 25 lbs per day per FLPMA. No commercial collection. No motorized excavation. No removal of artifacts or old mining structures. Pan or hand-tool collection only.
rules:

No motorized excavation

nearest_services: Coffee, CA (approximately 8 miles) - minimal services. Redding, CA (approximately 50 miles) - full services including gas, food, and supplies.
accessibility_notes: Gentle sloping terrain accessible to most hikers and families. Unpaved road to access point requires high-clearance vehicle. Once at the site, walking is straightforward on open slopes. Not suitable for wheelchair access due to road conditions and terrain. Sturdy footwear recommended.

faq.question: Can I find gold at Golden Jubilee Mine in Trinity County?
faq.answer: Yes. Golden Jubilee Mine is a historic gold prospect where rockhounds successfully pan for fine gold and dust gold in stream gravels. The site sits within California's northern gold belt, and small flakes are commonly recovered. Focus your panning efforts in drainage channels and benches where water naturally concentrates heavy materials.

faq.question: Is a permit required for rockhounding at Golden Jubilee Mine?
faq.answer: No permit is required at Golden Jubilee Mine. It is located on public BLM land open to personal use collection under Federal Land Policy and Management Act guidelines. You only need to follow the 25-pound-per-day limit and avoid motorized excavation.

faq.question: What is the best time of year to go gold panning near Coffee, California?
faq.answer: May through October is the best season. Trinity County's higher elevation means snow and difficult access conditions occur November through April. Summer months offer reliable dry weather, passable road conditions, and moderate temperatures ideal for outdoor prospecting.

faq.question: What tools do I need to rockhound at Golden Jubilee Mine?
faq.answer: For gold panning, bring a 10-14 inch gold pan and a classifier to efficiently process stream gravels. If hunting quartz specimens on hillsides, pack a small geology hammer and chisel. Always bring plenty of water, sunscreen, and a first aid kit. The remote location has no services nearby.

faq.question: Is Golden Jubilee Mine suitable for beginners and families?
faq.answer: Yes, it is beginner-friendly. The gentle slopes and open terrain make prospecting accessible to most skill levels, and children as young as 6 can participate in gold panning. No special experience is required. However, it is a remote location, so come fully prepared with water and supplies.

faq.question: Are there quartz specimens to collect at Golden Jubilee Mine besides gold?
faq.answer: Yes. Quartz specimens with visible mineralization are common in exposed banks and hillside outcrops throughout the area. Look for specimens with iron staining or glassy clarity on the surrounding slopes. The site's location in the Klamath Mountains quartz vein belt makes it productive for quality specimens.

meta_description: Free BLM gold panning near Coffee, CA. Find gold and quartz on gentle slopes. No permit required, 25 lbs per day limit. Beginner-friendly Trinity County site.
